description: Phase 1 P0 diagnostic baseline — 50 queries across 5 categories

memories.content: I always prefer dark mode in all editors and IDEs
Use 4-space indentation, never tabs
I prefer Python over JavaScript for backend work
Always use type hints in Python code
I like using pytest over unittest for testing
Use async/await whenever possible, avoid synchronous I/O
I prefer vim keybindings in all editors
Use conventional commits format for git messages
I prefer YAML over JSON for configuration files
Use uv instead of pip for Python package management
User is a senior backend engineer at TechCorp, 8 years experience
User's primary programming languages are Python and Go
User works on the OpenCortex project, a memory system for AI agents
User's timezone is Asia/Shanghai, UTC+8
User's name is Hugo and he speaks both Chinese and English
User uses macOS as primary development platform with M2 chip
User has a background in distributed systems and vector databases
User is the lead maintainer of the OpenCortex open-source project
User's development setup includes Cursor IDE and iTerm2
User has experience with Qdrant, Milvus, and Pinecone vector databases
OpenCortex uses Qdrant as its vector storage backend, running in embedded local mode
The HierarchicalRetriever implements wave-based frontier batching search with RL score fusion
CortexFS is the three-layer filesystem: L0 abstract, L1 overview, L2 content
IntentRouter has 3-layer query analysis: keywords, LLM, memory triggers
The MCP server is a pure Node.js stdio proxy with 9 tools, zero external dependencies
memories.abstract: MCP server: Node.js stdio proxy, 9 tools

memories.content: TraceSplitter uses LLM to decompose conversations into task-level traces
The scoring formula is: fused = beta * rerank + (1-beta) * retrieval + reward_weight * reward + hot_weight * hotness
BM25SparseEmbedder is integrated via CompositeHybridEmbedder wrapping all embedding providers
The project structure: src/opencortex/ contains config, orchestrator, http/, context/, storage/, retrieve/, alpha/, ingest/, parse/
ContextManager implements three-phase Memory Context Protocol: prepare, commit, end
2026-03-12: Fixed IntentRouter cache key bug — was missing session_context in key, causing stale results
2026-03-10: Completed legacy cleanup — deleted SessionManager, MemoryExtractor, ACE client headers
2026-03-08: Integrated local embedding (BGE-M3 ONNX via FastEmbed) and reranker (jina-reranker-v2-base-multilingual)
2026-03-05: Migrated 6 files from json to orjson for faster serialization
2026-03-14: Started Phase 1 optimization planning — reviewed design docs, identified 6 GPT-5.4 findings
2026-03-01: Deployed document scan batch import — ParserRegistry, MarkdownParser, heading-based chunking
2026-03-11: Fixed ContextManager._end() trying to extract knowledge_candidates from session_end() — key always missing
2026-02-28: Ran LoCoMo benchmark — OpenCortex overall F1=0.22 vs baseline F1=0.34
2026-03-09: Centralized 9 prompts into src/opencortex/prompts.py — zero internal deps, pure leaf module
2026-03-13: Reviewed Phase 1 optimization plan with GPT-5.4 — fixed constraint merge, baseline timing, dedup scope
memories.abstract: GPT-5.4 review findings applied to plan

memories.content: Error QDRANT_COLLECTION_NOT_FOUND (code 404) means the collection was not initialized — call init() on storage adapter first
Config file paths: server config at ~/.opencortex/server.json, client config at ~/.opencortex/mcp.json
The embedding dimension for multilingual-e5-large is 1024, for BGE-M3 it is 1024
URI format: opencortex://{team}/user/{uid}/{type}/{category}/{node_id}
Python entry point: uv run opencortex-server --host 127.0.0.1 --port 8921
The _HOTNESS_LAMBDA constant is math.log(2)/7.0 giving a 7-day half-life for hotness decay
RequestContextMiddleware parses X-Tenant-ID and X-User-ID from HTTP headers into contextvars
The file src/opencortex/storage/qdrant/filter_translator.py translates VikingDB DSL into Qdrant Filter objects
SCORE_PROPAGATION_ALPHA = 0.5 controls parent score propagation in hierarchical search
Docker: docker compose up -d starts the OpenCortex server container
